factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield hit 5.19%, highest since before 2008 financial crisis. Long-duration growth stocks (Nasdaq-heavy) are acutely sensitive to real yield moves; higher nominal yields compress tech multiples directly.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Persistent inflation concerns (Iran energy shock, Hormuz closure, Fed's Paulson flagging 'super-elevated' risks) are driving real yields higher. Tech mega-caps trade at premium multiples that erode sharply in high real-rate environments.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Repricing
factors.summary: Markets now pricing material probability of rate hikes by July 2027; Paulson noted 'risks super-elevated' and hike 'on table' if growth surges. ECB also signaling June hike potential if Hormuz remains closed. Hawkish repricing is negative for equities.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran / Hormuz)
factors.summary: Unresolved Hormuz Strait closure driving oil volatility, inflation fears, and safe-haven demand. Trump's repeated Iran strike threats add uncertainty; this reduces risk appetite and weighs on speculative tech positioning.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar Index rallying on strong ADP jobs data (42,250 avg 4-week adds, strongest since Oct 2025) and higher yields. Strong USD headwind for Nasdaq constituents with heavy international revenue exposure (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, etc.).

factors.name: Tech Sector Underperformance
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell -0.84% on the day; Russell 2000 -1.01%; S&P 500 -0.67%. Nasdaq's larger decline signals tech/growth taking disproportionate hit from yield move; leading indices suggest continued outflow risk.

factors.name: Crypto Correlates Weakening
factors.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $77k with ETF outflows exceeding $1B. BTC weakness typically correlates with Nasdaq risk-off sentiment; suggests broader momentum fade in growth/speculative assets.

factors.name: AI Capex and Earnings Optimism
factors.summary: SpaceX picks Goldman Sachs for mega-IPO; Google unveiling new Gemini Omni AI models; OpenAI announcing Guaranteed Capacity offering. Hyperscaler capex cycle remains robust, supporting mega-cap earnings narratives (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA).

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: Toll Brothers beat Q2 and raised FY guidance (up 5% post-market); Home Depot held FY outlook despite consumer headwinds. Selective earnings beats show underlying corporate fundamentals haven't cracked yet.

key_levels.support: 28600–28700 (4h MA support, prior session low area)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 is facing a structural headwind from surging Treasury yields (30Y at 5.19%, a 15+ year high) and rising real yields driven by persistent inflation concerns linked to the Iran-Hormuz energy shock and hawkish Fed repricing. Despite solid AI capex narratives and selective earnings beats, long-duration growth stocks are acutely vulnerable to multiple compression in a higher-yield regime. The move lower (-0.60% in 4h, following -0.84% daily in Nasdaq) is in early-to-mid phase; further yields moves or geopolitical escalation could cascade into risk-off cascade.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (June 11) — if Hormuz remains closed, June hike now seen as 'unavoidable' per Kocher; markets may reprice sooner
Fed's Paulson remarks continuing (speaking 2300 GMT May 20) — hawkish inflation talk risks another yield spike
FOMC meeting (late May / early June cycle) — market pricing of rate path a critical focus
Crude oil / energy data Wednesday — any Hormuz developments or Trump Iran announcements
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — next major catalyst for yield repricing
Mega-cap earnings (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, NVDA, TSLA, META) — forward guidance on capex and margin pressures in high-rate scenario
screening.risk_warning: If Hormuz tensions ease, oil/inflation fears deflate, and 10Y yields pull back below 4.5%, Nasdaq could reverse sharply higher. Alternatively, if mega-cap earnings beats remain resilient and hyperscaler AI capex guidance stays robust, multiple compression may be arrested. Fed pivot or dovish signaling would also flip bias bullish. Monitor 4h closes below 28600 as potential capitulation or 2900+ resistance break as short-squeeze risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (underway, not extended yet). Consider shorts on retest of 28900–29000 resistance with stop above 29200. Alternatively, wait for a 2–3% pullback retest of 28400–28600 support before fresh shorts. Avoid chasing lower; yield volatility could create whipsaws.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.19%, highest since before 2008 financial crisis per Bloomberg. Long-duration tech extremely sensitive to rising real rates, compressing growth multiples. Large block sales in Treasury futures exacerbated selloff.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Bets Rising
factors.summary: Philadelphia Fed's Paulson stated risks are 'super-elevated' and a hike is on the table if growth surges or inflation risks emerge. Markets now pricing in scenarios of extended hold or further rate hikes, with prediction markets seeing increasing odds of hike by July 2027.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike Signals
factors.summary: ECB's Kocher warned June rate hike unavoidable if Hormuz stays shut; Nagel said ECB may have to act in June as Iran energy shock proves persistent. Global central bank hawkishness increases real rate pressure on long-duration assets.

factors.name: Iran Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Trump threatened to 'give Iran another hit' with reports of strike options briefed. WSJ reported little progress in Iran talks. Mediators see no meaningful progress, keeping oil prices elevated and inflation fears high, supporting higher-for-longer rate narrative.

factors.name: Tech Sector Mixed Signals
factors.summary: Positive: SpaceX picks Goldman for record-breaking IPO, Google debuts new AI models and agents, Alphabet director sells $412K stock. Negative: No major mega-cap earnings catalyst in window, Astera Labs gains but broader tech sentiment pressured by rates.

factors.name: Equity Market Internals Weak
factors.summary: Major US indices closed lower: Nasdaq -0.84%, S&P 500 -0.67%, Russell 2000 -1.01% per investingLive. Small-cap and tech-led declines suggest risk-off positioning amid yield concerns and geopolitical uncertainty.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index strengthened toward 99.30 on strong ADP jobs data showing 42,250 jobs added over four weeks, strongest since series began. NDX components have heavy international revenue exposure, making stronger dollar a headwind.

factors.name: Japan Bond Market Stress
factors.summary: Japanese super-long yields at multi-year highs with 20-year JGB sale on Wednesday creating anxiety. Japan stocks under pressure as rates approach 3%, stoking fears of hit to corporate earnings. Global bond selloff contagion effect on risk assets.

factors.name: Inflation Concerns Persistent
factors.summary: Fed minutes may show businesses passing on higher energy costs. Oil edged lower on Iran progress headlines but WTI clawed back 'Hormuz premium.' Private crude inventory survey showed headline draw much greater than expected, keeping energy inflation risk elevated.

factors.name: Options Market Activity
factors.summary: Massive options bet on Brent crude plunging rattled oil traders. While not directly equity-related, unusual flows and heightened volatility in energy markets suggest elevated hedging demand and risk-off positioning across asset classes.

confirmation.narrative: Nasdaq 100 faces mounting pressure from surging Treasury yields (30Y at 5.19%, highest since 2008) and rising global rate hike bets. Fed's Paulson warned risks are 'super-elevated' with hikes on table, while ECB officials signaled June move unavoidable if Iran tensions persist. The confluence of deteriorating bond market conditions, persistent inflation fears from Iran war, and stronger dollar creates toxic environment for long-duration tech. Yesterday's -0.84% Nasdaq decline confirms Phase 2 weakness, with small-cap underperformance (-1.01% Russell) suggesting broad risk-off rotation.
catalysts_ahead: Japan 20-year JGB auction Wednesday (global bond market contagion risk)
Fed speakers including Paulson remarks at 2300 GMT
PBoC rate setting Wednesday (Asia session)
Weekly crude inventory data (official DOE report)
Any Iran war escalation headlines (Trump strike decision)
ECB June 11 meeting now pricing in potential hike
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: 1) Iran breakthrough announced with Hormuz reopening, collapsing oil and yields; 2) Treasury yields reverse sharply below 5.0% on 30Y; 3) Fed speakers push back aggressively on hike speculation; 4) Major mega-cap (NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL) announces surprise positive catalyst. Current move is Phase 2 (already down -0.84% on Nasdaq), not fresh catalyst.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway with Tuesday's -0.84% Nasdaq decline; wait for retest of 29,000-29,100 resistance or overnight bounce before considering short entries, as chasing here carries elevated risk of short-term oversold bounce.

factors.name: Global Bond Yield Surge
factors.summary: Long-maturity Treasury yields climbing to highest in ~2 decades per Bloomberg; strategists warn losses have room to run. Long-duration growth multiples compress sharply on rising nominal rates.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS Pressure
factors.summary: Persistent inflation concerns (Iran energy shock, supply disruptions) keep real yields elevated. Tech, already extended duration asset, faces headwind from real rate compression reversal.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Philadelphia Fed's Paulson (May 19) signals rate hike scenario 'on table' if growth surges or inflation risks emerge; ECB's Nagel flags June action likely. Markets pricing in extended hold or further hikes, not cuts.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Tensions)
factors.summary: Trump threatens to resume Iran strikes 'in 2-3 days' if no deal; Hormuz supply disruption fears persist. Risk-off sentiment, flight to safety, weaker risk appetite for equities; oil elevated ($103-110 range).

factors.name: Asia Tech Weakness
factors.summary: Samsung Electronics 47k+ workers striking Thu (wage collapse); KOSPI sinking. Semiconductor supply chain stress, broader Asia equity sell-off on Middle East and tech sector concerns.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: DXY near 99.50 (6-week high); safe-haven flows into USD on geopolitical/rate uncertainty. Strong dollar headwind for NAS100 multinationals (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN have heavy international revenue).

factors.name: China Policy Shift / Weak Stimulus Signal
factors.summary: PBOC leaves LPRs unchanged (12th consecutive hold), scrapping SME loan targets; signals shift to market-driven credit. Weak growth signal, fragile recovery narrative undermines China-linked Nasdaq plays.

factors.name: AI / Capex Momentum Mixed
factors.summary: Nvidia Singapore hub, OpenAI/Google deals in Asia positive; Alibaba new Zhenwu chip positive. But no mega-cap earnings yet; forward guidance on hyperscaler capex pending. Awaiting NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL prints.

factors.name: Recent Price Action (4h, 24h)
factors.summary: 4h change -0.60%, 24h +0.73%. Modest intraday weakness; longer-term drift mixed. Move not yet extended, but bias history shows prior SELL signal (Haiku 7/10 bearish → Sonnet 7/10 bearish on May 20).

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 faces a powerful headwind from surging long-duration real and nominal yields, exacerbated by persistent Iran-US tensions and ECB/Fed hawkish repricing. The bond market selloff—yields at 2-decade highs—directly compresses tech valuations (mega-cap growth multiples are duration-heavy). Concurrent Asia tech weakness (Samsung strike, KOSPI decline) and stronger USD add pressure on multinational earnings. While AI capex signals remain constructive, they are not sufficient to offset macro yield/rate headwinds until hyperscaler guidance clarifies. Prior SELL bias remains in effect; move is in early-to-mid stage, not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: UK CPI (May 20 06:00 GMT) — inflation persistence could reinforce hawkish Fed view, pressuring NQ further
Australia April Jobs (May 21-22) — RBA rate path signals; soft print could ease policy, supportive
FOMC Minutes (May 21 18:00 ET) — major event; market expecting hawkish tone on inflation/rate risks
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA) — timing TBD; forward guidance on capex, capex spending, margins critical
Iran-US ceasefire/escalation updates — every 6-12h; high volatility driver for yields and risk sentiment
Japan BOJ June meeting (signal of tapering pause/slowdown) — May 22-23 if announced; impacts global yield curve
EU tariff deal implementation (provisional agreement reached) — reduces headline trade war risk but geopolitical uncertainty remains
screening.risk_warning: Move would be invalidated by: (1) Sudden Iran-US ceasefire deal reducing geopolitical risk premium and yields; (2) Hawkish Fed pivot reversed by inflation miss (CPI/PCE surprise down); (3) Mega-cap earnings/guidance beat expectations on AI capex and cloud demand, reigniting growth narrative; (4) Bank of Japan slowing tapering, re-anchoring global yields lower. If yields reverse >20bps lower or risk-off sentiment lifts abruptly, bias flips bullish.
screening.entry_guidance: Move in early Phase 2; await retest of 28900-29000 resistance or pullback to 28500-28600 support before confirming short entry. Major FOMC Minutes (May 21) and UK CPI (May 20 early) will likely spike volatility—consider holding for clearer post-data structure before size.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields (10Y/30Y)
factors.summary: 10Y yields at highest since early 2025; 30Y yields hit post-2007 highs per Deutsche Bank. Long-duration growth stocks like NQ100 constituents extremely sensitive to real rate compression.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US tensions)
factors.summary: Iran threatens to extend conflict 'beyond region'; risk-off sentiment depressing equities. Strait of Hormuz constraints driving oil >$110, energy shocks rippling through macro expectations.

factors.name: Inflation & ECB Hawkish Signals
factors.summary: Euro area CPI confirmed +3.0% YoY (vs +2.6% prior); ECB policymaker Wunsch warns 'beginning of inflation problem' and June hike 'quite likely' if war persists. Stagflation fears rising.

factors.name: USD Strength & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: DXY pushing against 99.44 highs; USD near 6-week highs. Strong dollar headwind for NQ100 multinationals (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) with ~40-50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Uncertainty
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes 'Nvidia earnings can affirm chip stock rally or spur market chaos.' Hyperscaler capex guidance critical but not yet released; earnings season risk remains unresolved.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain (Samsung, ASML)
factors.summary: Samsung workers on brink of 48,000-strong strike starting Thursday; labor disruption risk in core NQ100 supply chain. ASML gains on UBS call but execution risk elevated.

factors.name: AI/Cloud Capex Demand
factors.summary: AI boom continues reshuffling global markets (Taiwan, S. Korea surge), but forward guidance from hyperscalers still awaited. Nvidia earnings are lynchpin; no new commentary in news window.

factors.name: Asia Market Weakness
factors.summary: Asia-Pacific indices fell on Wednesday amid elevated yields and geopolitical tensions. Nasdaq 100 futures showed only +0.51% overnight, trailing S&P 500 futures (+0.16%), signaling relative weakness in growth.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS Proxy)
factors.summary: Nominal yields surging faster than inflation expectations; real rates rising sharply. Long-duration growth tech extremely vulnerable as real rates move from negative to neutral/positive.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior reads (May 20 00:05: SELL 7/10 → Sonnet BEARISH 7/10; May 20 05:05: SELL 6/10) show sustained bearish lean. No confirmation trigger yet but bias is consistent across cycles.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a macro headwind: surging real yields (10Y at early-2025 highs, 30Y at post-2007 extremes), intensifying geopolitical risk (Iran-US escalation), and hawkish ECB rhetoric (June hike likely if war continues) are all headwinds for duration-sensitive mega-cap tech. While AI narratives remain intact, the forward-guidance catalyst (NVIDIA earnings, hyperscaler capex commentary) is still pending, leaving the index vulnerable to yield repricing and risk-off flows. The prior SELL bias has held through two consecutive reads, and NQ weakness vs. S&P 500 futures overnight (-35 bps relative) signals rotation out of pure growth into value/defensives.
catalysts_ahead: NVIDIA earnings (date not specified in news but imminent based on Bloomberg emphasis)
FOMC Minutes (expected soon; will clarify rate-hike path in light of inflation/geopolitical shock)
US-Iran ceasefire negotiations (binary risk; breakthrough could flip safe-haven flows)
ECB June policy decision (early June; Wunsch suggests tightening likely if conflict persists)
Samsung strike outcome (Thursday, May 21; supply chain impact)
Microsoft/Google/Amazon earnings and cloud capex guidance (date TBD; critical for hyperscaler demand narrative)
screening.risk_warning: Sharp US-Iran de-escalation or ceasefire announcement would reverse safe-haven flows, weaken USD, and lower yields, reigniting risk-on sentiment and tech upside (28,900+ quickly). Alternatively, if NVIDIA/hyperscaler earnings beat expectations and reaffirm capex trajectory despite macro headwinds, growth could re-rate higher and invalidate the short bias. Real yield compression (if inflation surprise to downside or Fed signals dovish pivot) would also flip the bias decisively bullish.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (shift underway post-geopolitical shock). Avoid chasing shorts into 4h weakness; wait for retest of 28,900–29,000 resistance or hold through FOMC Minutes / NVIDIA earnings (binary catalysts). If resistance breaks cleanly, thesis invalidates; if rejected, lower levels (28,600–28,700) offer better risk/reward for continuations.

factors.name: NVDA earnings catalyst (imminent)
factors.summary: Markets are pricing in NVIDIA earnings as a pivotal near-term catalyst. Pre-market movers show chip stocks rebounding ahead of results; semiconductor momentum on HBM demand (Samsung, SK Hynix gains noted) suggests positive sentiment into print.

factors.name: Retail earnings beat streak
factors.summary: Target, Lowe's, and TJX all beat expectations and raised guidance. Retail turnaround narrative contradicts UBS warning on consumer slowdown, but recent data is constructive on demand resilience.

factors.name: Real yields / TIPS pressure
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields surging (30-year at 2007 highs per Investinglive), inflation concerns elevated, Fed rate-hike odds at 50% by year-end. Rising real yields are headwind for high-duration growth/tech multiples.

factors.name: Fed hawkish repricing
factors.summary: Markets pricing Fed tightening risk amid US-Iran tensions, supply disruptions (Strait of Hormuz closure), crude >$102. FOMC minutes due; potential for further hawkish surprise if committee signals easing bias removal.

factors.name: US Dollar strength
factors.summary: DXY testing six-week highs near 99.40, USD/JPY back toward 160, EUR/USD at fresh six-week lows (1.1590). Strong dollar headwind for Nasdaq mega-caps with heavy international revenue (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN).

factors.name: Consumer slowdown warning (UBS)
factors.summary: UBS Chief Strategist Bhanu Baweja warns real disposable income growth nearing zero and fiscal support fading threaten equity rally. Counters AI-driven earnings beat narrative with macro demand risk.

factors.name: China growth deceleration
factors.summary: China scaled back government spending at fastest pace in 6 months; unexpected slowdown across economy (Bloomberg). Demand shock risk for semiconductor and cloud capex cycles tied to Chinese data centers.

factors.name: Crypto risk-on correlation
factors.summary: BTC hovering near $77,000 with fading momentum; support at $74-76k at risk. Crypto weakness signals caution on equity risk-appetite, but equity weakness not yet confirmed by BTC collapse.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran/Ukraine/Taiwan)
factors.summary: US-Iran stalemate prolonging; Xi-Putin meeting underway; tech supply-chain risk (Taiwan absent from Xi-Trump discussions). Elevated binary tail risk in energy, semiconductors, and defense supply chain.

factors.name: AI capex and hyperscaler outlook
factors.summary: No new guidance in news window; market awaiting NVIDIA earnings and cloud platform commentary (AWS, Azure, GCP) from hyperscalers. Bristol Myers deploying Claude AI suggests enterprise adoption, but scope limited.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a classic conflict: AI mega-cap earnings (especially NVDA tonight) support a relief bounce, but bond yields are surging on inflation fears and Fed hawkish repricing, real yields rising, and the dollar strengthening—all headwinds for growth multiples. Retail beat streak (Target, Lowe's, TJX) suggests consumer resilience, but UBS macro call on income slowdown and China spending cuts add recession risk. The index has bounced +0.73% in 24h but is down 0.60% in 4h, suggesting momentum stalling into NVDA print. This is a PHASE_2 bounce off recent lows, not a sustained uptrend; conviction is tempered by near-term event risk and structural yield headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: NVIDIA earnings (tonight/imminent) — single largest NDX mover; guidance on AI demand and capex critical
FOMC minutes release (likely May 21–22) — clarity on easing-bias removal and committee hawkishness
CPI / PCE inflation data (late May) — will reset Fed rate expectations; key for yield and multiple repricing
Hyperscaler earnings (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, AAPL in coming weeks) — cloud capex guidance essential
US-Iran ceasefire resolution (binary event) — could unlock energy/growth risk-on if resolved, cap-ex risk if escalates
China stimulus or data revision — could stabilize semiconductor demand outlook
screening.risk_warning: NVDA miss or weak guidance would reverse this BUY bias sharply (SELL 7–8). Conversely, NVDA beat + bullish cloud capex outlook + any Fed dovish pivot (yield reversal below 4.5% on 10-year) would extend rally to 29,200+. Watch for gap-fill or retest of 28,500 support if NVDA disappoints—that would signal continuation of selloff to 28,200–28,000.
screening.entry_guidance: NAS100 is in mid-bounce before NVDA print; avoid chasing here. Best entry on retest of 28,600–28,700 support if NVDA misses or if 10-year yields reverse below 4.4%; alternatively, wait for post-earnings volatility fade and a +0.5–1% gap up if NVDA crushes and raises guidance.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Talks (Risk-On)
factors.summary: Trump announced talks in 'final stages' with Iran, causing immediate rally in risk assets. Oil fell from $106 to $98-100, USD weakened (AUDUSD +0.76%, GBP/USD gains), and equities surged. S&P 500 +0.94%, NASDAQ +1.31% on the news.

factors.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield Pullback
factors.summary: 10Y yield dropped ~10 bps to 4.57% from 4.687% peak yesterday. Lower nominal rates are structurally bullish for high-duration growth/tech stocks that dominate NQ. Real yields also easing on the selloff.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Strength
factors.summary: Semis rebounding hard post-weakness: NVDA +1.71%, AMD +7.53%. Micron boosted by Samsung supply-tightness expectations (looming strike). Bernstein raised WFE (wafer fab equipment) forecasts, lifting LRCX and KLAX targets.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: US Dollar Index softer on Iran peace hopes and yield compression. Weaker USD typically supports international revenue streams for mega-cap tech (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL), which are ~45% of NQ weighting.

factors.name: AI Capex & Data Center Tailwinds
factors.summary: Power firms jumped on news of major US grid accelerating data-center pairing timelines. Crypto/fintech (Mercury up to $5.2B valuation) and AI infrastructure narrative remains strong. Broadcom, CrowdStrike, and cloud operators in focus.

factors.summary: Target beat estimates and raised FY guidance (retail resilience signal). Lowe's beat on challenging housing backdrop. Corporate earnings beat rate remains elevated, reducing recession fears for growth stocks.

factors.name: Fed Minutes Today (2 PM ET)
factors.summary: April 28-29 FOMC minutes due this afternoon. Market already pricing modest dovish lean (yield down today). If minutes show no hawkish surprise, bias holds. High risk of repricing if inflation language is sterner.

factors.name: ECB June Rate Hike Signal
factors.summary: Reuters reporting ECB rate hike 'nearly sealed' for June due to persistent energy prices and inflation. This could pressure EUR (bearish for USD) and raise global real rates slightly, pressuring duration assets in the short term.

factors.name: Options Skew: NVDA Unusual Positioning
factors.summary: Short-dated NVDA calls trading at premium to puts ahead of earnings, and implied move estimates have overestimated post-report swings 6 of 7 quarters. Earnings volatility may be priced in; execution risk remains.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Escalation Tail)
factors.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatened to extend conflict 'beyond the region' if US/Israel resume attacks. Trump said he's 'in no hurry,' but rhetoric remains hot. Sharp reversal in risk-on sentiment if talks collapse.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early PHASE_2 of a risk-on rally triggered by Trump's 'final stages' Iran peace announcement. Geopolitical de-escalation hopes, coupled with a 10-bps Treasury yield pullback and weaker USD, have reignited appetite for high-duration growth and semiconductor exposure. Mega-cap tech beneficiaries (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) are showing renewed momentum as recession fears ease and AI capex narrative stays intact. However, this move is ~1.3% into NQ's daily gains already, and the Fed FOMC minutes release this afternoon at 2 PM ET presents a repricing risk if inflation language surprises hawkish.

ECB June rate hike confirmation or any hawkish surprise (likely this week)
NVDA earnings (timing unclear from news, but high market sensitivity)

screening.risk_warning: 1) Fed FOMC minutes show unexpectedly hawkish inflation language → yields spike, NQ corrects 1–2%. 2) Iran talks collapse or new attack headlines → oil rallies, risk-off flows crush growth/semis. 3) NVDA or MSFT miss earnings/guidance → single-name volatility could cascade across NQ. 4) ECB hawkish surprise in June guidance → global real rates rise, pressuring duration. Move is already extended after strong morning gains; late-stage chasing into this rally carries elevated pullback risk.

factors.summary: Reuters sources cite ECB June rate hike 'nearly sealed' due to persistent energy-driven inflation. Euro weakness (EUR/USD, EUR/GBP down) but ECB tightening adds global rate pressure—potential headwind for US tech multiples if contagion occurs.

factors.summary: April FOMC minutes release pending. Market pricing remains hawkish post-recent labor data. Any incremental hawkish tilt could cap rally; dovish shift unlikely but would boost.

factors.summary: S&P +0.94%, NASDAQ +1.31% intraday per Investinglive. European markets closed strong (DAX +1.36%, CAC +1.70%). Global risk-on flows rotating into tech.

factors.summary: USD turned negative on Iran news; USD/JPY broke below 100-hour MA at 158.78. Weaker dollar benefits NDX (heavy international revenue exposure) and eases financial conditions.

factors.name: Real Yields (Implicit)
factors.summary: Nominal yields down 10 bps with inflation expectations likely softening (oil down, peace hopes). Real yield decline is powerful tailwind for long-duration growth stocks in NDX.

factors.name: Target/Lowe's Retail Beats
factors.summary: Both retailers beat estimates, but Target shares fell on skepticism. Mixed consumer signal; not a direct NDX driver but indicates selective optimism in sectors outside tech.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 is rallying on a powerful risk-on shift driven by Trump's 'final stages' Iran talks comment, which crushed oil prices and removed near-term stagflation fears. The 10 bp drop in 10-year yields (4.57%) is critical—lower discount rates directly boost NDX's long-duration tech valuations. Semiconductors (NVDA +1.71%, AMD +7.53%) are rebounding after recent weakness, supported by Bernstein upgrades and Samsung supply concerns. However, the move is already underway (+1.31% NASDAQ intraday), placing this in Phase 2. NVDA earnings and Fed minutes (2 PM ET) are immediate catalysts that could extend or reverse the rally.

Fed FOMC minutes release (2 PM ET today, May 20)
ECB June meeting (hawkish tilt could ripple into US rates)

confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) US-Iran talks collapse and oil spikes back above $105; (2) Fed minutes reveal more hawkish inflation concerns than priced, sending 10Y yield back above 4.65%; (3) NVDA earnings disappoint on guidance, triggering 3-5% NDX drop; (4) Rally extends without pullback into Fed minutes, creating overextended long positioning vulnerable to headline risk.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: NVDA earnings catalyst imminent
factors.summary: Nvidia earnings expected imminently; chip stocks rallying ahead with short-dated calls trading at premium to puts (MarketWatch). Single-name NVDA move can swing NQ 2-3%; positive setup in options market.

factors.name: Fed hawkish surprise (FOMC minutes)
factors.summary: Fed minutes (May 20, 18:00 ET) revealed majority of officials signaling rate hikes likely if inflation stays elevated; many preferred removing easing bias from policy statement. This is a fresh hawkish repricing and headwind for long-duration growth tech.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yields declining
factors.summary: US 10Y yield down ~10 bps to 4.57% (from 4.687% yesterday per FXStreet 16:07). Lower nominal yields support equity multiples; relief trade after hawkish Fed surprise shows tactical buying in duration-sensitive growth.

factors.name: Risk-on sentiment (Iran deal hopes)
factors.summary: Trump signaled US-Iran talks in 'final stages'; oil crashed >5% (WTI below $100/bbl), equities rallied, USD weakened, AUD surged. Broad risk-on mood benefits mega-cap tech with international revenue exposure.

factors.name: AI IPO momentum (OpenAI filing imminent)
factors.summary: OpenAI preparing confidential IPO filing as soon as Friday, targeting September debut (Bloomberg/WSJ 16:48, 17:35). Investor appetite for AI unicorns and anticipated mega-cap offerings bolster sentiment for Nasdaq 100 component stocks.

factors.name: Meta, Google, Alphabet strategic positioning
factors.summary: Meta began ~10% workforce layoffs (8,000 jobs) signaling AI-era discipline; Google I/O results show rapid Gemini adoption and AI monetization (BofA sees 10% upside per Decrypt 18:04). Hyperscaler optionality supports sector.

factors.name: Inflation persistence (AI-driven cost pressures)
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports AI boom is boosting inflation despite expectations of price reductions; alongside oil volatility and tariff residuals, 3-year inflation highs create persistent real-yield headwind for growth equities. Real rates likely remain sticky.

factors.name: Execution risk on mega-cap earnings
factors.summary: NVDA earnings imminent; Lowe's flagged cost pressures; Target cautious on guidance despite strong sales (comp +5.6%). Earnings execution critical; any disappointment on capex or guidance could reverse rally.

factors.name: Antitrust/regulatory overhang
factors.summary: BlackRock private credit fund DOJ probe (Bloomberg 19:08); continued scrutiny on mega-cap valuations and lending practices. Regulatory friction remains a tail risk, though not immediately market-moving.

factors.name: Chinese AI competitive threat
factors.summary: US Top News 17:47 reports Chinese AI labs matching American frontier capability at fraction of cost; potential margin/valuation pressure on US AI giants and cost-of-capital implications. Long-term structural concern.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain resilience
factors.summary: Samsung avoided strike via pay deal (18:49); ASML/TSMC supply steady. No acute supply shocks, but China export controls remain baseline risk.

factors.name: Dollar weakness (risk-on tailwind)
factors.summary: USD/CHF weakened, GBP/USD rallied >0.30% on Iran deal hopes and falling yields (FXStreet 18:03, 15:56). Weaker dollar benefits US multinational earnings translation for mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Equity fund flows and QQQ positioning
factors.summary: No explicit ETF flow data in news; however, AI IPO appetite and mega-cap positioning suggest institutional accumulation bias. Implied but not directly confirmed.

factors.name: VIX/volatility (options skew)
factors.summary: NVDA short-dated calls at premium to puts suggests elevated vol and bullish tilt into earnings, but risk/reward tightening. Market pricing in large move; not immediately actionable directional signal.

key_levels.resistance: 29,400–29,500 (recent rally high, NVDA earnings breakout zone)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between two competing forces: (1) fresh hawkish Fed surprise from April 28-29 FOMC minutes (majority signaling rate hikes if inflation persists), which weighs on long-duration growth multiples and real yields; and (2) powerful risk-on catalyst from Trump's Iran deal commentary, which slashed oil >5%, weakened the dollar, and reignited appetite for mega-cap tech. The imminent NVDA earnings and OpenAI IPO filing momentum provide near-term bullish narrative hooks. However, the Fed's inflation warning and AI-driven cost pressures create a structural undertow. The rally is modest (+0.38% in 4h, +0.52% in 24h) and shows PHASE 2 dynamics—the move has started but not extended—suggesting further upside is possible if NVDA beats and Iran talks solidify, but sentiment remains fragile heading into earnings.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA Q1 earnings (expected May 22–23, imminent per multiple sources)
MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, AMZN earnings (late May / early June)
OpenAI IPO confidential filing (potentially Friday May 24)
US economic data: CPI (early June), PCE, NFP (early June)
Iran deal outcome / geopolitical resolution
Next FOMC meeting minutes or guidance shift
screening.risk_warning: Hawkish Fed surprise from FOMC minutes could re-accelerate real-yield repricing if inflation remains sticky; any disappointment in NVDA earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance could trigger sharp reversal. Chinese AI competitive cost pressure is a longer-term margin headwind. If Iran deal stalls, oil rally resumes and inflation expectations re-anchor higher, forcing Fed pivot back to hiking, which would crush growth multiples. Execution risk on mega-cap guidance is HIGH ahead of earnings season.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE 2 (+0.5% in 24h); wait for NVDA earnings print and retest of 29,100–29,150 support zone before adding long exposure. If earnings beat and Iran deal progresses, break above 29,400 targets 29,600+. Short only on failure to hold 29,000 on close or major macro surprise (inflation/Fed hawkish reversal).
